element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Core puzzle-action synchronization requiring timing coordination
Vertical space navigation with gate timing

rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Stop-start action with synchronization-based timing pauses; fast-paced co-op when Controller 2 used instead of R.O.B. due to R.O.B.'s slow mechanical response

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introduction to gate mechanics and basic hazard avoidance; manageable with coordination
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Increased complexity in gate timing sequences and enemy patterns
notable_spikes: Gate synchronization difficulty when using R.O.B. (described as 'hard as hell' requiring 'two or three moves ahead' thinking)
Sustained R.O.B. gameplay fatigue (noted as not suitable for extended play sessions like '40 hours')

notable_level_patterns.description: Player must time movement with gate openings/closings, either controlled by R.O.B. or second controller. Creates synchronization-based tension and relief cycles.
Climbing sequences requiring navigation through gates and around enemies in vertical space

sources_used: wikipedia - R.O.B. article (Development and Hardware sections with designer quotes)

metadata.notes: Sources provide hardware context and design intent but limited explicit level-by-level documentation. Spatial and rhythm patterns inferred from game mechanic descriptions (R.O.B. gate control, vertical climbing, synchronization timing). Difficulty insights from developer quotes about gameplay challenge and R.O.B. mechanical slowness affecting pacing.
